title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The open-source vulnerability scanner itself poses minimal direct risk, but its widespread adoption could lead to potential misuse by malicious actors to identify exploitable vulnerabilities in unpatched systems.
recommended_actions: Evaluate the tool in a controlled environment to ensure it aligns with organizational security policies.
Monitor for any updates or reports of misuse of the tool in the cybersecurity community.
Educate development and security teams on secure usage practices for open-source vulnerability scanning tools.

title: The UnderConstructionPage PRO plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Arbitrary File Read in all...
description: The UnderConstructionPage PRO plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Arbitrary File Read in all versions up to, and including, 5.76. This is due to the plugin accepting arbitrary local file paths in the template_thumbnail parameter and copying their contents into a publicly accessible uploads file. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Subscriber-level access and above, to read arbitrary files on the server, which can contain sensitive information.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated attackers with minimal privileges (Subscriber-level access) to read arbitrary files on the server, potentially exposing sensitive information such as configuration files, database credentials, or user data. This could lead to further exploitation or data breaches.
recommended_actions: Update the UnderConstructionPage PRO plugin to the latest patched version as soon as it becomes available.
Restrict access to Subscriber-level accounts and review permissions to minimize exposure to potential attackers.
Monitor server logs for unusual activity, especially file access patterns, and implement Web Application Firewall (WAF) rules to detect and block exploitation attempts.

title: The WP Grid Builder plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Privilege Escalation in all versions up...
description: The WP Grid Builder plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Privilege Escalation in all versions up to, and including, 2.3.3. This is due to missing authorization and meta key validation in the `update()` handler for the `/wp-json/wpgb/v2/metadata` REST endpoint. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Subscriber-level access and above, to elevate their privileges to Administrator by updating their own `wp_capabilities` user meta with a crafted nested array payload.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-13756.

title:
OpenPLC Runtime v3 contains an authenticated arbitrary file write 
vulnerability in the legacy...
description:
OpenPLC Runtime v3 contains an authenticated arbitrary file write 
vulnerability in the legacy web UI program‑upload workflow. The 
application stores an attacker‑supplied filename (prog_file) directly 
into the Programs.File database field and later uses this value as the 
destination path for an uploaded file without validating or restricting 
the path. Because Python os.path.join() honors attacker‑controlled 
absolute paths, an authenticated user can write arbitrary files anywhere
 writable b

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: This vulnerability allows an authenticated attacker to write arbitrary files to any writable location on the server, potentially leading to remote code execution, data corruption, or unauthorized access to sensitive files.
recommended_actions: Immediately update OpenPLC Runtime to the latest patched version if available.
Restrict access to the legacy web UI and ensure strong authentication mechanisms are in place.
Implement file path validation and sanitization to prevent arbitrary file writes.

title: CVE-2025-71372 - Picklescan before 0.0.33 fails to detect the numpy.f2py.crackfortran.getlincoef gadget in pickle __r
description: Picklescan before 0.0.33 fails to detect the numpy.f2py.crackfortran.getlincoef gadget in pickle __reduce__ methods, allowing arbitrary code execution. Attackers can craft malicious pickle files that execute arbitrary Python code when loaded, bypassing Picklescan's safety checks and enabling supply-chain poisoning of shared model files.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to execute arbitrary Python code by exploiting a flaw in the Picklescan library's detection mechanism, potentially leading to supply-chain attacks, data breaches, and unauthorized access to systems relying on shared pickle files.
recommended_actions: Upgrade Picklescan to version 0.0.33 or later to ensure detection of malicious pickle files.
Implement additional validation and sanitization mechanisms for pickle files before loading them, such as using alternative serialization methods like JSON or Protobuf.
Educate developers and users about the risks of loading untrusted pickle files and enforce strict policies around file sharing and supply-chain security.

title: CVE-2025-71369 - picklescan before 0.0.28 fails to detect malicious pickle files that use torch.utils.data.datapipes.
description: picklescan before 0.0.28 fails to detect malicious pickle files that use torch.utils.data.datapipes.utils.decoder.basichandlers in reduce methods, allowing attackers to bypass safety checks. Remote attackers can embed undetected malicious code in pickle files that executes during deserialization, enabling remote code execution.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2025-71369.

title: CVE-2025-71367 - picklescan before 0.0.34 fails to detect _operator.attrgetter function calls in pickle payloads, all
description: picklescan before 0.0.34 fails to detect _operator.attrgetter function calls in pickle payloads, allowing attackers to bypass security checks. Remote attackers can craft malicious pickle files using _operator.attrgetter in reduce methods to execute arbitrary code when pickle.load() processes the file.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in picklescan before version 0.0.34 allows attackers to bypass security checks and execute arbitrary code by exploiting the _operator.attrgetter function in pickle payloads. This could lead to unauthorized access, data breaches, or system compromise if malicious pickle files are processed.
recommended_actions: Immediately update picklescan to version 0.0.34 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Implement strict input validation and avoid deserializing untrusted pickle files to minimize exposure to malicious payloads.
Conduct a security audit of systems and applications that rely on pickle deserialization to identify potential risks.

title: CVE-2026-54424 - An Incorrect Use of Privileged APIs vulnerability in Unity Parsec on Windows hosts leads to a potent
description: An Incorrect Use of Privileged APIs vulnerability in Unity Parsec on Windows hosts leads to a potential Elevation of Privilege. This issue affects Parsec through v2026-05-04.0. The patched version is Parsec for Windows version 150-104a. A user can generate a situation where there is an instance of parsecd.exe running as NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM with a user-controlled value of the AppData environment variable.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-54424.
